title: resume.parsed payload (v1)
description: Emitted when the resume parse+enrich+verify pipeline finishes for one resume. Producer: resume-service (apps/resume-service/src/lib/parse-pipeline.ts). The REAL payload is a superset of the typed ResumeParsedPayloadSchema in packages/contracts: it also ships the full `parsed`, `enriched`, `githubCorroboration` and `verification` blobs that candidate-service and search-service read directly off the payload (not just the summary counts).
x-consumers: screening-service (enqueue one screening per ACTIVE application)
job-service apply-ingest-subscriber (advance accept-fast ledger row to PARSED)

tenantId.description: Owning tenant. Mandatory per the multi-tenancy mandate (also on the envelope).

resumeId.description: Resume record id (not necessarily a UUID).

bulkUploadId.description: Set when the resume came from a bulk archive ingest; null for a single upload / public apply.

parseCostUsd.description: LLM cost of the resume-parser run (snapshot.costUsd).

parsed.description: The raw resume-parser agent output (result.output). Consumed by candidate-service as a fallback when `enriched` is absent, and by search-service. Free-form parsed-resume shape (identity fields may be { value, confidence } wrapped).

enriched.description: The enriched view (taxonomy canonicalization, per-skill YOE, normalized dates, evidence-adjusted confidence). candidate-service prefers this over `parsed` for parsedSummary; search-service reads skills/summary from it.

githubCorroboration.description: GitHub-corroboration agent output, or null when no GitHub handle / corroboration disabled.

verification.description: Agentic resume-verifier output (trust score + findings + trace), or null when the verifier did not run.
